page: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and includes advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2074.0 and a multi-core score of 15951.0, both falling into the strong tier. Passmark CPU testing yielded a score of 48904.2, with strong results in encryption and physics simulations. Single-threaded gzip compression reached 33.6 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput was measured at 51.12 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the hardware-assisted instruction sets enabled prompt processing speeds of 2246.87 tokens per second and text generation speeds of 286.26 tokens per second on a small 135M parameter model. However, performance scales down to average levels for larger models, with a 7B parameter model achieving 18.95 tokens per second for text generation, and 70B parameter models are unsupported due to memory constraints.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c7i-flex.8xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high network throughput. The lack of bundled local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which may introduce latency tradeoffs. The baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps is relatively low for an instance of this size, potentially limiting its utility in distributed data-intensive clusters. This instance is suited for tasks such as software compilation, image processing, text processing, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor. It provides 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show strong performance in single-core tasks, image processing, and web serving, alongside average multi-core and database execution. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. This profile makes it cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not demand high-speed local storage or extreme network throughput, such as software compilation, text processing, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i-flex.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon 8488C processors.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring DDR5 memory and Intel AMX instructions for efficient processing and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor.
64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Strong benchmark performance in single-core execution, image processing, and web serving.
No bundled local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.
Suited for software compilation, text processing, and small-scale model inference.
